title: Erdős #349 sublemma: every natural is a sum of distinct powers of 2
informal_statement: Every natural number k is a sum of distinct powers of 2 over some finite index set E: for all k, there exists a Finset E of naturals such that k = sum over i in E of 2^i (binary representation existence). This is a sublemma supporting the integer_isGoodPair_iff characterization used toward Erdos Problem #349, not a resolution of #349 itself (which asks, in general, for which real t, alpha the sequence floor(t*alpha^n) is additively complete -- still genuinely OPEN).
informal_proof_idea: Direct construction: take E to be the set of bit positions of k (k.bitIndices.toFinset), then k equals the sum of 2^i over that index set by Mathlib's Finset.sum_toFinset_bitIndices_two_pow.
formal_statement_pp: theorem erdos_349_exists_finset_sum_two_pow (k : ℕ) : ∃ E : Finset ℕ, k = ∑ i ∈ E, 2 ^ i

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop; root_statement_hash confirmed byte-identical to the upstream formal-conjectures source hash cited in the sibling repo's provenance comment

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only an infrastructure sublemma used toward the integer_isGoodPair_iff characterization, which itself is a companion/partial result for Erdos #349's INTEGER case -- it does not resolve Erdos #349's general open question (for which real t, alpha is floor(t*alpha^n) additively complete), which the sibling repo's own whitepaper explicitly states remains OPEN and is not attacked by this line of work. Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode a6801189, problem_version 420dd077-4bed-4b31-835e-6f47617b3e40), a single Solve step, kernel_verified on the first attempt.
